title: Read and write files with fs module
description: Read a text file asynchronously, append some text, and write back to a new file.
explanation: The fs module provides file system operations. Prefer fs.promises for cleaner async/await code instead of callback-based APIs.

title: Create a basic HTTP server
description: Create an HTTP server that responds with 'Hello World' for GET / and JSON for GET /api/users.
hints: req.method and req.url identify the route
explanation: Node.js's built-in http module creates servers. In practice, use Express.js for routing. res.writeHead sets status codes and headers, res.end sends the response body.

title: Build a REST API with Express
description: Create a CRUD REST API for a todo list using Express with GET, POST, PUT, DELETE endpoints.
explanation: Express simplifies HTTP routing. Use express.json() middleware to parse request bodies. RESTful conventions: 201 for created, 204 for deleted, 404 for not found.

title: Create custom Express middleware
description: Write a request logger middleware, authentication middleware, and error handling middleware.
hints: Middleware calls next() to pass to next handler
Error middleware has 4 parameters (err, req, res, next)
Order matters — register error handler last
explanation: Express middleware are functions that process requests. They access req, res, and next. Call next() to continue, next(err) to pass to error handler. Register error middleware last with 4 params.

title: Process large files with streams
description: Read a large CSV file using streams, process each line, and pipe to output without loading entire file in memory.
hints: Streams process data chunk by chunk
for await...of works with async iterables
explanation: Streams handle large data without loading everything into memory. Readable streams emit data events, writable streams accept data. Use pipeline for safe stream composition with automatic error handling and cleanup.

title: CPU-intensive task with Worker Threads
description: Move CPU-intensive fibonacci calculation to a Worker Thread to avoid blocking the event loop.
hints: Worker threads share no memory by default
explanation: Node.js is single-threaded, but Worker Threads allow true parallelism for CPU-intensive tasks. Unlike child_process, workers share memory via SharedArrayBuffer.

title: Build a custom EventEmitter
description: Create a class extending EventEmitter to build an order processing system.
hints: Extend EventEmitter to inherit event methods
explanation: Node.js EventEmitter is the foundation of its event-driven architecture. Extending it lets your classes emit and listen to custom events, enabling decoupled, reactive systems.

title: Scale with Node.js Cluster
description: Use the cluster module to take advantage of multiple CPU cores.
explanation: Node.js Cluster forks worker processes, each running on a CPU core. They share the TCP connection, so incoming requests are load-balanced across workers. Dead workers are automatically restarted.
